channel.title: Hermes Atlas — new projects
channel.description: Newly added community-built tools, skills, plugins, and integrations for Nous Research's Hermes Agent. Updated daily.

item.title: hermes-nextcloud (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and stori

item.title: honcho (Memory & Context)
item.description: Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to build stateful agents that track evolving relationships between users, groups, and projects. It utilizes a background reasoning loop to extract conclusions and peer representations from stored conversation logs and events rather than relying solely on simple vector matching. Developers can query these representations for natural-language insights or inject session-specific context directly into LLM prompts. The system supports both a managed

item.title: hermeshq (Deployment & Infra)
item.description: HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to e

item.title: learn-hermes-agent (Guides & Docs)
item.description: Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementation

item.title: hermes-agent-guide (Guides & Docs)
item.description: The Hermes Agent Guide is a comprehensive Chinese-language documentation project designed to bridge the gap between fragmented information and practical implementation of the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book knowledge map covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory systems, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details technical workflows for local and cloud deployment, MCP protocol automation, and multi-platform connectivity with s

item.title: agent-browser-mcp (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, inc

item.title: ContextPilot (Memory & Context)
item.description: ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing prefix cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping data blocks before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant KV cache recomputation and lowers token consumption without compromising reasoning accuracy. The project provides native plugins for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, and supports backends like vLLM, SGLang

item.title: super-hermes (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis

item.title: portable-hermes-agent (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker, administrator rights, or system-wide installations. It operates through an embedded Python environment and connects to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The platform integrates over 100 tools for tasks including image generation via ComfyUI, text-to-speech, music creation, and automated multi-step workflows. Us

item.title: SkillClaw (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: SkillClaw is an agentic skill management system that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It operates as a background process that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, allowing knowledge gained in one context to benefit others. It natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and various OpenAI

item.title: mnemosyne (Memory & Context)
item.description: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed for the Hermes Agent framework to provide private, zero-dependency context management. It utilizes SQLite with native vector search and full-text search to store conversations and knowledge without requiring external databases or API keys. The system operates entirely in-process to eliminate network latency and supports offline use cases. It features a three-tier architecture consisting of working, episodic, and scratchpad memory for long-term in

item.title: keep (Memory & Context)
item.description: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that enables long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media files—by summarizing, embedding, and organizing them into a navigable graph of "edge tags." The system automatically surfaces relevant past learnings and commitments during agent sessions to improve decision-making. It supports fully local operation via Ollama or MLX, as well as cloud-hosted and API-based configurations.

item.title: hermes-web-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a graphical interface for AI interaction and system administration. The platform connects directly to the Hermes state database and configuration files to manage chat sessions, provider credentials, and multi-platform communication channels. Users can monitor real-time usage analytics, schedule automated cron jobs, and manage files across local or remote backends like Docker and SSH. It also featur

item.title: hermes-otel (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: The hermes-otel plugin provides observability for Hermes Agent by automatically exporting execution data as OpenTelemetry spans. It captures LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests, sending them to OTLP-compatible backends for monitoring and debugging. The plugin supports a wide range of backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Jaeger for tracing, metrics, and logs. Users can configure multiple backends simultaneously through a YAML configuration file to fan out data across

item.title: hermes-telegram-miniapp (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within the Telegram ecosystem. It establishes a secure mobile-first workspace by wrapping the Hermes CLI in a dark-mode web UI, featuring streaming chat and system monitoring. The application utilizes dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure secure access while providing tools for cron job management and background agent spawning. Users can also perform visio

item.title: hermes-relay (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: Hermes-Relay is a companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent through a native Android app and a desktop CLI. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay to bridge local devices with remote agent instances, allowing for persistent sessions across different platforms. The Android application provides mobile device control including screen reading and gesture execution, while the desktop client allows a remote agent to execute local terminal commands and file ope

item.title: hermes-lcm (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that compacts older messages into depth-aware summary nodes stored in a local SQLite database. This architecture allows the agent to maintain a bounded active context while using specialized tools to drill back into raw message history or specific summary branches. The system also i

item.title: hermesclaw (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI models and agents using simple chat commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /three. The tool also supports Ope

item.title: hermes-claude-code-rc (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and

item.title: hermes-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web dashboard designed to serve as the centralized command center for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It functions as a single-file React application served via a lightweight Python proxy, enabling users to interact with their self-hosted assistant without complex frontend deployments. The interface provides comprehensive visibility into agent operations through real-time log streaming, a task board for tracking active tool work, and integrated file browsing. It also feat

item.title: gbrain (Memory & Context)
item.description: GBrain is a persistent memory and knowledge graph system designed to prevent AI agents from losing context over time. It utilizes a self-wiring graph layer and hybrid search to extract entity relationships like investments and affiliations without requiring additional LLM calls. The system consolidates data from meetings, emails, and tweets into a structured timeline with backlink-boosted ranking for improved retrieval accuracy. It supports 14 embedding providers and includes built-in benchmarki

item.title: hermes-webui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes WebUI is a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, providing a graphical alternative to the standard command-line experience. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, it maintains full parity with CLI features without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent settings through a centralized control center. It allows users to interact with persistent agent memory, manage

item.title: clawshell (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provid

item.title: hermes-desktop (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a focused workbench for managing Hermes Agent hosts. It connects directly via SSH to interact with remote sessions, files, and workflows without requiring a browser wrapper or local data synchronization. Users can manage Kanban boards, edit skills, and monitor usage across multiple agent profiles from a single interface. The app also includes an embedded terminal and local workflow presets to launch tasks directly on the remote host.

item.title: hermes-ecosystem (Guides & Docs)
item.description: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub sta

item.title: hermes-control-interface (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time terminal access, multi-agent gateway management, and live log streaming. Users can configure over 80 agent settings, monitor token analytics and costs, and manage scheduled cron jobs across multiple profiles. The platform also includes a secure file explorer, a revamped ch

item.title: hermes-skill-distillation (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from the Hermes agent and converts them into high-quality training data via a specialized environment and scoring system. The pipeline supports both offline SFT data generation and live reinforcement learning through integration with the Atropos training framework. By focusing on practical tasks like coding and system administration, it cr

item.title: hermes-alpha (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities o

item.title: hermes-agent-camel (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: This project is a version of the Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL-style trust boundaries to protect against indirect prompt injection. It implements an opt-in runtime guard that monitors or enforces security policies by gating sensitive tool side effects against verified user intent. The system maintains the core Hermes features, including a self-improving learning loop, autonomous skill creation, and cross-platform accessibility via Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Users can toggle between monit

item.title: HermesWiki (Guides & Docs)
item.description: HermesWiki serves as the central documentation hub for the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides a structured repository of information to help users understand and implement the agent's capabilities. The wiki contains specific deployment patterns and practical recipes for various email automation scenarios. By centralizing these resources, it assists developers in configuring and scaling their AI-driven communication workflows.
